author: draganrakita
content:
Left a option [here](https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1504670887851327648/1520016266532884562) that could unify all of this, it is becoming messy. 

tldr: charge bear minimum as intrinsic gas, and charge additional gas in runtime (halt if oog and tx still valid)

author: draganrakita
content: Wdym this is part of EIP-2780 <:shipit:636891691927732224>

author: lu_pinto
content: So if I understood correctly in your view intrinsic gas at mempool validation should cover the bare minimum for authorization lists? Like no state writing, only ecrecover and tx bytes? Is that correct?

author: draganrakita
content: Yes, that is the idea, and other part is done in runtime.

author: lu_pinto
content:
Ok because I'm not sure how that helps the mempool validation. So you are not failing as early as possible anymore. You have to execute the tx to check if the sender has money to pay for the state and db writing.
I think we need to validate against the worst case always because:
- user needs to have money to cover for the state part and this needs to be checked without executing. Otherwise it makes mempool validation more difficult.
- the compute part for writing to the DB is something that is not charged in advance of doing the actual work and user must have funds to pay for it in advance of doing the actual work in EVM.

author: draganrakita
content: mempool has less checks to do in this way, and it is simpler for protocol.

author: draganrakita
content: Only thing that we must charge and check are base tx gas (inclusion of tx bytes in block) and recoveries, other things are additions.

author: draganrakita
content: If user want to be sure that worst case is charged, we should tweek gas estimators and wallets to put worst value. That is more elegant reasoning imo.

author: draganrakita
content: And thinking about frames, this would be only thing that you could do as you dont know worst case.
